subject: refactor(content): per-cell structured text, remove textCells indirection
body:
Previously, structured text had one global set of fields (title, tagline,
body, CTA, footnote) assigned to cells via a separate textCells mapping.
This was indirect and confusing for non-technical users who had to find
a cell assignment dropdown on each text element.

Now each cell gets its own set of structured text fields. Both Guided and
Freeform modes share the same cell selector — pick a cell, edit its content.
The textCells state field and all its plumbing are removed entirely.

Changes:
- useAdState: text[cellIndex][elementId] shape, setText(cellIndex, layer, updates)
- AdCanvas: simplified getElementsForCell reads per-cell text directly
- ContentTab: unified cell selector for both modes, no cell assignment UI
- LayoutTab: removed textCells validation and props
- layoutPresets: removed textCells from all 26 presets
- cellUtils: text cleanup instead of textCells cleanup
- Migration: old saves auto-converted via migrateTextToPerCell()

Tags: content, state, ux, simplification
Complexity: 4
Urgency: 2
Impact: user-facing
Risk: medium
Debt: paid
Epic: content-editing
Semver: major
